page: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd is a memory-optimized and storage-intensive server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor. This x86_64 architecture system features 144 physical cores and 288 dedicated vCPUs operating at a base frequency of 2.3 GHz. It is equipped with 2232 GB of RAM, providing approximately 7.75 GB of memory per core. The server includes 48 local NVMe SSDs, delivering a total of 19031 GB of high-speed local storage. No GPUs are included in this configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates top-tier performance, ranking in the top 10% for multi-core CPU execution, database operations, and memory bandwidth. Specifically, it achieves a read-only large block memory bandwidth of 1060.25 GB/sec and a write large block bandwidth of 411.36 GB/sec. It also excels in cryptographic operations, software compilation, and static web serving throughput. However, a notable performance tradeoff is observed in LLM inference text generation, where it registers weak performance for small models and average performance for large models, though prompt processing speeds remain strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the server offers high resource density by combining massive memory capacity with extensive local NVMe storage on a single node, reducing the need for external storage arrays. This hardware profile is optimized for large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, heavy multi-threaded decompression, and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd is a high-capacity server featuring 288 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor, 2232 GB of RAM, and 19031 GB of local NVMe SSD storage across 48 drives. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, this instance delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and memory bandwidth, with read-only large block speeds reaching 1060.25 GB/sec. While it excels in data-intensive tasks, software compilation, and cryptography, it exhibits a performance tradeoff in LLM text generation, which ranges from weak to average. The qualitative cost efficiency of this server is defined by its high resource density, consolidating massive compute, memory, and local storage. It is highly suited for large-scale databases, heavy multi-threaded data processing, and high-throughput web serving.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd features 288 vCPUs, 2232 GB RAM, and 19031 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. It delivers top-tier database, memory bandwidth, and multi-core CPU performance.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-highmem-288-lssd is a high-capacity server with 288 vCPUs, 2232 GB RAM, and 19031 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.
tagline: A high-density compute and storage platform featuring massive memory capacity and local NVMe SSDs for data-intensive enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 288 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 6985P-C processor
2232 GB of RAM paired with 19031 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier multi-core CPU, database, and memory bandwidth performance
Tradeoff in LLM text generation speeds compared to prompt processing
Best suited for large-scale databases, web serving, and heavy data processing
